arts.ttl: Arsenal injury news: Bukayo Saka, Martin Odegaard, Gabriel Martinelli and Kai Havertz updates
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta gave updates on Bukayo Saka, Kai Havertz, Gabriel Martinelli, Martin Odegaard, Ben White, Oleksandr Zinchenko, Jurrien Timber, Takehiro Tomiyasu and Thomas Partey

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta gives compelling case on why Thomas Tuchel deserved England job
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta said his English Arsenal players had a big smile on their faces when Thomas Tuchel was unveiled by the FA on Wednesday despite groans over appointment of a foreigner to top job

arts.ttl: Source: Wilshere to leave Arsenal for Norwich role
arts.sum: Former England midfielder Jack Wilshere is set to join Norwich City as a first-team coach, sources have told ESPN.

arts.ttl: ‘Unbelievable’: Ben White says £27m Arsenal player’s technique in training is absolutely ridiculous
arts.sum: David Raya joined Arsenal last summer from Brentford, and he came in at a strange time. Indeed, Raya was coming in to replace a goalkeeper...

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta provides huge Arsenal injury update on Saka and seven others ahead of Bournemouth
arts.sum: “He’s not gonna be fit for this game,” Arteta said. There was better news on Thomas Partey who Arteta confirmed will be fit for the game.

arts.ttl: Former Arsenal winger Nicolas Pépé reveals he almost made Lille return
arts.sum: Nicolas Pépé (29) has revealed that he almost made a return to former club Lille OSC this summer, before ultimately opting to join Villarreal in La Liga. Pépé made 79…

arts.ttl: Bournemouth vs Arsenal predictions: Gunners to land another shutout win
arts.sum: Arsenal have a been a rock-solid defensive unit for some time and Simon Barlow expects that to continue and lead to a victory at Bournemouth at teatime on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Riccardo Calafiori's Arsenal transfer admission speaks volumes about Mikel Arteta
arts.sum: Arsenal signed Italy international Riccardo Calafiori from Bologna this summer in a £42million deal and the versatile defender has needed no time to settle into his new surroundings

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta rules key Arsenal player out of Bournemouth clash as huge injury update provided
arts.sum: When asked if Odegaard could play a part in Arsenal's next seven games, before the November international break, the Gunners boss replied: "Hopefully, yes." Mikel Arteta has confirmed Arsenal captain Martin Odegaard will not be fit for the weekend clash against Bournemouth.

arts.ttl: Nine players missing from Arsenal training explained after major boost for Bournemouth trip
arts.sum: Martin Odegaard, Oleksandr Zinchenko, Ben White, Jurrien Timber, Kai Havertz, Thomas Partey, Kieran Tierney, Gabriel Martinelli and Bukayo Saka are not clipped up in the selection made available to fans. Lastly, Odegaard is confirmed out for Bournemouth but Arteta committed to seeing a return in the next seven games before the next international break but no word yet on Kieran Tierney.
